narrativeProgression.summary: Unknown contact reveals personal detail only the player would recognize.
narrativeProgression.beatNarrative: A timed message threatens collateral harm unless the party diverts.

threadUpdates.update: Ultimatum Window: A timed message threatens collateral harm unless the party diverts.
Peripheral signal tied to Abandoned node scan remains unresolved.
Cross-day mismatch suggests archival tampering tied to Abandoned node scan.
A plausible shortcut appears: Disable network vs exploit protocol for leverage.
NPC language mirrors player choices with unsettling precision.

evidenceDrops.title: Dead Channel Protocol - Day 20 crime scene photo
evidenceDrops.summary: Artifact appears authoritative but contains one intentional inconsistency tied to Abandoned node scan.

imagePrompt.style: hyper-realistic forensic horror, analog imperfection, grounded true-crime framing
imagePrompt.prompt: Dead Channel Protocol ARG artifact Day 20. Type: crime_scene_photo. Clue focus: Abandoned node scan. Location: Smart-city control grid and abandoned transit nodes. Phase: Danger & Direct Involvement. Camera: Canon EOS R6, 35mm prime, ISO 3200, f/1.8, 1/40 shutter. Visual defects: sensor noise, halation bloom, slight motion blur, fingerprint smudge, timestamp burn-in. Lighting: practical sodium-vapor spill with deep shadow falloff, imperfect white balance. Composition: evidence-forward framing, handwritten marks, believable wear, no cinematic polish. Subtle misleading cue included.
imagePrompt.negativePrompt: clean CGI, fantasy look, glossy studio lighting, over-sharpened edges, watermark, logo, gore fetish, cartoon style

audioPrompt.style: psychological dread, industrial ambience, noisy field recording authenticity
audioPrompt.prompt: Dead Channel Protocol crime_scene_photo audio cue for day 20. Clue anchor: Abandoned node scan. Phase: Danger & Direct Involvement. Texture: low drone bed, distant metallic resonance, intermittent room-tone dropouts. Include one decipherable spoken fragment and one environmental clue (HVAC hum, rail click, siren bleed, pager chirp). Add mild tape flutter, codec smear, clipped transients, and uneven dynamic floor to keep it grounded.

interactions.messageTemplate: {{player_alias}}, before you move: chain-of-custody on today's drop is unstable. Confirm Abandoned node scan and tell me if your last call still stands (decision.day_19.primary).
I heard my own words on a line I never called. If you still trust {{trusted_contact}}, do not forward this to the wrong desk.
Off-record: I can publish tonight, but your previous choice is already circulating. I need one verification point tied to Abandoned node scan.
You chose {{last_major_choice}} and still think this is about evidence. Check the mirror timestamp and decide who you are willing to sacrifice.

puzzle.title: Day 20 Puzzle
puzzle.objective: Use the new evidence and prior logs to resolve contradiction around Abandoned node scan.
instructions: Extract timestamp, sender route, and one lexical anomaly from today's artifacts.
Compare against at least one artifact from a previous day.
Submit a concise deduction statement before unlocking the next high-risk contact.
hintLadder: Hint 1: One timestamp appears valid but is offset from device timezone.
Hint 2: Cross-check sender metadata against yesterday's call-log route.
Hint 3: The answer keyword starts with ABAN.
puzzle.failureConsequence: Antagonist sends a personalized pressure message and witness trust drops.

awarenessMoments.trigger: if decision.log contains any entry for previous day
awarenessMoments.lineTemplate: You repeated yesterday's pattern ({{last_major_choice}}). People who survive this case learn faster.

awarenessMoments.trigger: if trusted contact changed within last 3 days
awarenessMoments.lineTemplate: Your channel loyalty shifted to {{trusted_contact}}. That tells me what you fear most.

adaptiveLines: Reference previous choice without breaking realism.
Acknowledge response latency as if monitored by live actors.
Escalate or soften tone based on trust flags and solved puzzles.
